categories.label: Adaptogens
categories.description: Herbs that help the body adapt to stress and support nervous system resilience

categories.label: Nervines & Calming Herbs
categories.description: Herbs that directly support and calm the nervous system

categories.label: Traditional Chinese Medicine
categories.description: TCM herbs and formulas for emotional balance and nervous system support

categories.label: Ayurvedic Herbs
categories.description: Ayurvedic herbs for nervous system support and emotional balance

categories.label: Supplements & Nutrients
categories.description: Evidence-based supplements for nervous system and trauma recovery support

categories.label: Books & Resources
categories.description: Essential reading for somatic healing and trauma recovery

categories.label: Somatic Tools & Devices
categories.description: Physical tools that support nervous system regulation

items.name: Ashwagandha (KSM-66)
items.description: The most studied adaptogen for cortisol reduction and nervous system resilience. KSM-66 is the highest-concentration full-spectrum ashwagandha extract, with 21 clinical trials showing significant reductions in stress, anxiety, and cortisol.
items.cautions: Avoid in thyroid conditions; may interact with thyroid medications

items.name: Rhodiola Rosea
items.description: A Siberian adaptogen with strong evidence for reducing burnout, fatigue, and stress-related cognitive impairment. Particularly effective for the sympathetic dominant nervous system.
items.cautions: Can be stimulating — take in the morning; avoid if bipolar

items.name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)
items.description: Sacred in Ayurveda, tulsi is a gentle adaptogen with anxiolytic and antidepressant properties. Particularly good for those with anxiety mixed with depression.
items.dosage: 300-600mg extract daily, or 1-2 cups of tulsi tea
items.cautions: Generally very safe; may slow blood clotting

items.name: Eleuthero (Siberian Ginseng)
items.description: One of the original adaptogens, studied extensively by Soviet scientists for stress resilience and performance under pressure. Gentle and well-tolerated.
items.cautions: Avoid with high blood pressure; may interact with blood thinners

items.name: Schisandra Berry
items.description: A TCM adaptogen that supports liver function, stress resilience, and mental clarity. Known as the 'five-flavor berry' for its complex taste and broad therapeutic effects.
items.cautions: May interact with some medications metabolized by the liver

items.name: Lion's Mane Mushroom
items.description: A medicinal mushroom with unique nerve growth factor (NGF) stimulating properties. Supports neuroplasticity, cognitive function, and has shown promise for anxiety and depression.
items.cautions: Generally very safe; rare allergic reactions

items.name: Reishi Mushroom
items.description: The 'mushroom of immortality' in TCM, reishi supports immune function, reduces stress, and promotes deep sleep. Particularly helpful for those with chronic stress and sleep disruption.
items.cautions: May thin blood; avoid before surgery

items.name: Passionflower
items.description: One of the most effective nervine herbs for anxiety and sleep. Works on GABA receptors similarly to benzodiazepines but without the addiction risk. Particularly effective for anxiety with racing thoughts.
items.dosage: 250-500mg extract, or 1-2 cups of tea before bed
items.cautions: May potentiate sedatives; avoid during pregnancy

items.name: Valerian Root
items.description: A classic nervine and sleep herb with evidence for reducing anxiety and improving sleep quality. Works best taken consistently over 2-4 weeks.
items.cautions: May cause vivid dreams; avoid with alcohol and sedatives

items.name: Lemon Balm
items.description: A gentle, pleasant nervine herb that reduces anxiety and improves mood. Combines beautifully with passionflower and valerian for sleep support.
items.dosage: 300-600mg extract, or 2-3 cups of tea daily
items.cautions: Very safe; may reduce thyroid hormone synthesis in high doses

items.name: Kava Kava
items.description: A traditional Pacific Islander herb with strong evidence for anxiety reduction. Works on GABA receptors. Use noble kava varieties only and cycle usage.
items.dosage: 70-250mg kavalactones daily; cycle 3 weeks on, 1 week off
items.cautions: Use only noble kava; avoid with alcohol and liver conditions; cycle usage

items.name: Skullcap
items.description: A North American nervine herb that calms nervous system overactivation, reduces anxiety, and supports sleep. Particularly effective for nervous tension and muscle spasm.
items.cautions: Generally very safe; ensure quality source (adulteration is common)

items.name: California Poppy
items.description: A gentle, non-addictive nervine related to the opium poppy but without any narcotic effects. Excellent for anxiety, pain, and sleep.
items.cautions: Very safe; avoid during pregnancy

items.name: Motherwort
items.description: A bitter nervine herb particularly effective for heart palpitations, anxiety, and the physical symptoms of stress. Especially helpful for those who carry anxiety in the chest.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy; may interact with heart medications

items.name: An Shen Bu Xin Wan
items.description: A classical TCM formula for 'calming the spirit and nourishing the heart.' Used for anxiety, insomnia, palpitations, and emotional instability from a TCM perspective of Heart Yin deficiency.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner for proper diagnosis and dosing

items.name: Xiao Yao Wan (Free and Easy Wanderer)
items.description: One of the most widely used TCM formulas for emotional health. Addresses Liver Qi stagnation — the TCM pattern associated with stress, frustration, depression, and PMS.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner; not for use during pregnancy

items.name: Gui Pi Wan (Restore the Spleen)
items.description: A TCM formula for anxiety, worry, insomnia, and fatigue from overthinking. Addresses Heart and Spleen deficiency — the pattern of exhaustion from chronic worry and emotional depletion.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner for proper diagnosis

items.name: Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
items.description: A TCM formula for insomnia, anxiety, and mental restlessness from Heart and Kidney Yin deficiency. Particularly effective for those who wake at night with racing thoughts.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner; contains herbs that may interact with medications

items.name: Bai Zi Yang Xin Wan
items.description: A TCM formula for nourishing the Heart and calming the spirit. Used for palpitations, anxiety, and emotional instability.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Jia Wei Xiao Yao Wan
items.description: An enhanced version of Xiao Yao Wan with added herbs for heat from stagnation. Particularly effective for anxiety with irritability, hot flashes, and emotional volatility.
benefits: Relieves Liver Qi stagnation with heat
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner; not for use during pregnancy

items.name: Long Dan Xie Gan Wan
items.description: A TCM formula for clearing Liver and Gallbladder heat. Used for acute anxiety, rage, headaches, and urinary tract issues from excess heat.
items.dosage: 8 pills 3x daily for short-term use only
items.cautions: Short-term use only; consult a TCM practitioner; contains gentian which can be harsh long-term

items.name: Suan Zao Ren Tang
items.description: A classical TCM formula for insomnia from Liver Blood deficiency and Heart Yin deficiency. One of the most used formulas for sleep in TCM practice.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Ashwagandha (Ayurvedic Grade)
items.description: In Ayurveda, ashwagandha is a Rasayana — a rejuvenating herb for the nervous system and vital essence. Traditional preparations use the root in warm milk (golden milk) for optimal absorption.
items.dosage: 1/2 tsp powder in warm milk with ghee and honey, or 300-600mg extract
items.cautions: Avoid in thyroid conditions; warming herb — may increase Pitta

items.name: Brahmi / Bacopa Monnieri
items.description: A premier Ayurvedic brain tonic with strong evidence for cognitive function, anxiety reduction, and neuroprotection. Particularly effective for stress-related cognitive impairment.
items.cautions: Takes 4-6 weeks for full effect; may cause digestive upset if taken without food

items.name: Shatavari
items.description: The premier Ayurvedic herb for female reproductive health and emotional nourishment. Deeply nourishing for the nervous system, particularly for those who feel depleted, dried out, or emotionally exhausted.
items.dosage: 500-1000mg daily, or 1/2 tsp powder in warm milk
items.cautions: Avoid in estrogen-sensitive conditions

items.name: Jatamansi (Spikenard)
items.description: A sacred Ayurvedic herb for the nervous system, used for anxiety, insomnia, and emotional trauma. Works on the mind and heart to bring deep calm and clarity.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy; may potentiate sedatives

items.name: Shankhpushpi
items.description: An Ayurvedic herb for memory, intelligence, and nervous system support. Used for anxiety, insomnia, and stress-related cognitive decline.
items.cautions: Generally safe; consult an Ayurvedic practitioner for dosing

items.name: Magnesium Glycinate
items.description: The most bioavailable form of magnesium for nervous system support. Magnesium deficiency is extremely common and directly linked to anxiety, insomnia, muscle tension, and hyperreactivity. This is often the single most impactful supplement for nervous system support.
items.cautions: Very safe; may cause loose stools at high doses — reduce dose if so

items.name: L-Theanine
items.description: An amino acid from green tea that promotes calm alertness without sedation. Increases alpha brain waves and GABA. One of the best supplements for anxiety without drowsiness.
items.dosage: 100-400mg daily; can be taken as needed
items.cautions: Very safe; may lower blood pressure

items.name: GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid)
items.description: The primary inhibitory neurotransmitter of the nervous system. Supplemental GABA has shown some evidence for reducing anxiety and improving sleep, though absorption across the blood-brain barrier is debated.
items.cautions: Generally safe; evidence for crossing blood-brain barrier is mixed

items.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil (High EPA)
items.description: High-EPA omega-3 fatty acids have strong evidence for reducing inflammation, supporting mood, and reducing anxiety. EPA is particularly important for emotional regulation and trauma recovery.
items.dosage: 1-3g EPA+DHA daily, with at least 60% EPA
items.cautions: May thin blood; use high-quality, tested brands to avoid heavy metals

items.name: Vitamin D3 + K2
items.description: Vitamin D deficiency is extremely common and strongly linked to depression, anxiety, and immune dysfunction. K2 is essential for directing calcium to bones rather than arteries.
items.cautions: Test levels before supplementing; excess D3 can be toxic

items.name: B-Complex (Methylated)
items.description: B vitamins are essential cofactors for neurotransmitter synthesis. Methylated forms (methylfolate, methylcobalamin) are important for those with MTHFR variants, which are common in trauma survivors.
items.cautions: Use methylated forms; high B6 can cause neuropathy at very high doses

items.name: Zinc Bisglycinate
items.description: Zinc is essential for GABA function, immune health, and wound healing. Deficiency is linked to anxiety, depression, and immune dysfunction. Bisglycinate is the most bioavailable form.
items.cautions: Take with food to avoid nausea; balance with copper; don't exceed 40mg/day

items.name: Phosphatidylserine
items.description: A phospholipid that supports cortisol regulation and cognitive function. Particularly effective for those with HPA axis dysregulation and cortisol excess.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may interact with blood thinners

items.name: 5-HTP (5-Hydroxytryptophan)
items.description: A direct precursor to serotonin. Has evidence for depression, anxiety, and sleep improvement. Use with caution if on SSRIs.
items.cautions: DO NOT combine with SSRIs, MAOIs, or triptans — serotonin syndrome risk

items.name: N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC)
items.description: A precursor to glutathione, the body's master antioxidant. NAC has emerging evidence for OCD, addiction, PTSD, and reducing neuroinflammation. Particularly useful for compulsive behaviors.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may interact with nitroglycerin

items.name: The Body Keeps the Score
items.description: Bessel van der Kolk's landmark book on trauma and the body. The most important book on trauma published in the last 30 years. Essential reading for anyone on a somatic healing journey.
benefits: Foundational understanding of trauma and the body
Explains why talk therapy alone isn't enough
items.author: Bessel van der Kolk, MD

items.name: Waking the Tiger: Healing Trauma
items.description: Peter Levine's foundational work on Somatic Experiencing. Explains how animals naturally discharge trauma and how humans can learn to do the same. Accessible and transformative.
items.author: Peter A. Levine

items.name: In an Unspoken Voice
items.description: Peter Levine's more comprehensive and clinical follow-up to Waking the Tiger. Deeper exploration of SE theory and practice.
items.author: Peter A. Levine

items.name: Trauma and Recovery
items.description: Judith Herman's classic work on complex trauma. Introduced the concept of complex PTSD and remains one of the most important books on trauma recovery.
benefits: Political and social context of trauma
items.author: Judith Herman, MD

items.name: The Polyvagal Theory
items.description: Stephen Porges' original academic text on polyvagal theory. More technical than his later works, but essential for understanding the science.
benefits: Foundation for understanding nervous system states
items.author: Stephen W. Porges

items.name: Polyvagal Exercises for Safety and Connection
items.description: Deb Dana's practical workbook for applying polyvagal theory in daily life. The most accessible and practical polyvagal book for non-clinicians.
items.author: Deb Dana

items.name: Anchored: How to Befriend Your Nervous System
items.description: Deb Dana's most accessible book on polyvagal theory. Warm, practical, and deeply helpful for anyone wanting to understand and work with their nervous system.
items.author: Deb Dana

items.name: Complex PTSD: From Surviving to Thriving
items.description: Pete Walker's essential guide to complex PTSD and the 4F responses (fight, flight, freeze, fawn). Written from lived experience and clinical practice. One of the most practical books on C-PTSD recovery.
items.author: Pete Walker

items.name: Healing Trauma: A Pioneering Program for Restoring the Wisdom of Your Body
items.description: Peter Levine's accessible, practical book with audio exercises for self-directed somatic healing. Excellent starting point for those new to somatic work.
items.author: Peter A. Levine

items.name: When the Body Says No: Exploring the Stress-Disease Connection
items.description: Gabor Maté's exploration of how suppressed emotions and chronic stress lead to physical disease. Essential reading for understanding somatic symptoms.
items.author: Gabor Maté, MD

items.name: Scattered Minds: The Origins and Healing of Attention Deficit Disorder
items.description: Gabor Maté's compassionate reframe of ADHD as a trauma response rather than a genetic disorder. Transformative for those who have been labeled with ADHD.
items.author: Gabor Maté, MD

items.name: No Bad Parts: Healing Trauma and Restoring Wholeness with IFS
items.description: Richard Schwartz's accessible introduction to Internal Family Systems (IFS). Transforms how you relate to your inner world and the parts of yourself that carry trauma.
items.author: Richard C. Schwartz, PhD

items.name: Getting Past Your Past: Take Control of Your Life with EMDR
items.description: Francine Shapiro's accessible guide to EMDR for non-clinicians. Explains how EMDR works and includes self-help techniques based on EMDR principles.
items.author: Francine Shapiro, PhD

items.name: Yoga Nidra Mat (Acupressure Mat)
items.description: Acupressure mats stimulate the nervous system through gentle pressure points, promoting relaxation, endorphin release, and nervous system regulation. Many trauma survivors find them deeply calming.
items.usage: Lie on mat for 20-30 minutes; start with a thin layer of clothing if too intense
items.cautions: Not for open wounds or skin conditions; start gently

items.name: Weighted Blanket (15-20 lb)
items.description: Deep pressure stimulation from weighted blankets activates the parasympathetic nervous system and increases serotonin and melatonin. Particularly effective for anxiety, sensory processing differences, and sleep.
items.usage: Use for sleep or during rest; choose 10% of body weight
items.cautions: Not for young children or those who cannot remove it themselves

items.name: Pulsetto Vagus Nerve Stimulator
items.description: A non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator that uses electrical pulses to stimulate the vagus nerve through the neck. Emerging evidence for anxiety, depression, and nervous system regulation.
items.usage: Follow manufacturer instructions; typically 4-minute sessions
items.cautions: Not for those with pacemakers, epilepsy, or certain heart conditions

items.name: Tibetan Singing Bowl
items.description: Singing bowls produce vibrations and tones that can support nervous system regulation through sound and vibration. Used in meditation, sound healing, and somatic practices.
items.usage: Strike or rim the bowl and allow the sound to wash over you; focus on the vibration
items.cautions: Very safe; choose quality bowls for best sound

items.name: Foam Roller (Deep Tissue)
items.description: Foam rolling releases myofascial tension, improves circulation, and can help discharge stored tension from the body. A practical somatic tool for daily use.
items.usage: Roll slowly over tense areas; pause on tender spots; breathe deeply
items.cautions: Avoid rolling directly on joints or the spine; not for acute injuries

items.name: Breathing Zone App (iOS/Android)
items.description: A biofeedback-based breathing app that guides you to your optimal breathing rate for HRV (heart rate variability) improvement. One of the most evidence-based tools for nervous system regulation.
items.usage: Use for 10-20 minutes daily; follow the visual breathing guide
items.cautions: Very safe; some people feel lightheaded initially — reduce session length

items.name: The Trauma Healing Workbook
items.description: A structured workbook for trauma healing with evidence-based exercises, journal prompts, and somatic practices. Designed for self-directed healing work.
items.usage: Work through at your own pace; don't push through if overwhelmed
items.cautions: Go slowly; have support available if needed

items.name: Cold Plunge Tub
items.description: Cold water immersion activates the vagus nerve, releases norepinephrine, and can rapidly shift nervous system state. Increasingly popular for mental health and nervous system regulation.
items.usage: Start with cold showers; work up to 2-5 minute cold immersion
items.cautions: Not for cardiovascular conditions; consult a doctor if in doubt; never alone

items.name: Red Light Therapy Panel
items.description: Red and near-infrared light therapy supports mitochondrial function, reduces inflammation, and has emerging evidence for mood and cognitive support.
items.usage: 10-20 minutes daily at 6-12 inches from the panel
items.cautions: Protect eyes; avoid if on photosensitizing medications

items.name: Cordyceps Mushroom
items.description: An adaptogenic mushroom that supports adrenal function, energy production, and stress resilience. Particularly helpful for those with fatigue from chronic stress.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may be stimulating — take in the morning

items.name: Maca Root
items.description: A Peruvian adaptogen that supports hormonal balance, energy, and mood. Particularly helpful for those with HPA axis dysregulation and hormonal imbalance.
items.cautions: Avoid in thyroid conditions; may be stimulating

items.name: Astragalus Root
items.description: A TCM adaptogen with strong immune-modulating and anti-aging properties. Supports the body during chronic stress and immune depletion.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid in autoimmune conditions

items.name: Panax Ginseng
items.description: The original adaptogen, with thousands of years of use. Strong evidence for cognitive function, stress resilience, and immune support.
items.cautions: Can be stimulating; avoid with blood thinners; cycle usage

items.name: American Ginseng
items.description: A gentler, more cooling ginseng compared to Panax. Better for those who run hot, anxious, or overstimulated. Supports cognitive function and stress resilience.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may lower blood sugar

items.name: Chaga Mushroom
items.description: A powerful antioxidant mushroom with immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory properties. Supports the body under chronic stress.
items.cautions: May interact with blood thinners and diabetes medications

items.name: Turkey Tail Mushroom
items.description: A medicinal mushroom with the strongest evidence for immune support. Contains PSK and PSP, which have been studied in cancer research.
items.cautions: Generally very safe

items.name: Dang Gui (Angelica Sinensis)
items.description: The premier TCM blood tonic herb, particularly for women. Nourishes Blood, regulates menstruation, and supports emotional health.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy; may thin blood

items.name: Bai Shao (White Peony Root)
items.description: A key TCM herb for nourishing Liver Blood and calming Liver Yang rising. Used for anxiety, irritability, muscle spasm, and menstrual irregularity.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Huang Qi (Astragalus)
items.description: The primary TCM Qi tonic herb. Strengthens Wei Qi (defensive energy), supports immune function, and builds resilience.
items.cautions: Avoid in acute infections; consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: He Shou Wu (Fo-Ti)
items.description: A TCM Kidney and Liver tonic herb known for anti-aging and blood nourishing properties. Use only prepared (processed) form.
items.dosage: As directed by TCM practitioner; use only prepared form
items.cautions: Use ONLY prepared (zhi) form; raw form is hepatotoxic; consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra)
items.description: The TCM name for schisandra berry. Used to calm the spirit, support the Heart, and nourish the Kidneys.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Fu Ling (Poria)
items.description: A TCM mushroom that calms the spirit, supports the Spleen, and resolves dampness. Used for anxiety, insomnia, and digestive weakness.
items.cautions: Generally safe; consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Yuan Zhi (Polygala Root)
items.description: A TCM herb that calms the spirit, opens the orifices of the Heart, and resolves phlegm. Used for anxiety, insomnia, and emotional confusion.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Suan Zao Ren (Zizyphus Seed)
items.description: The primary TCM herb for insomnia. Nourishes Heart Blood and calms the spirit.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Chai Hu Shu Gan San
items.description: A TCM formula for Liver Qi stagnation with pain. Used for depression, frustration, and pain from emotional stagnation.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Ban Xia Hou Po Tang
items.description: A TCM formula for the sensation of something stuck in the throat (plum pit qi) — anxiety with throat tightness.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Wen Dan Tang (Warm Gallbladder)
items.description: A TCM formula for anxiety, insomnia, and palpitations from Gallbladder deficiency. Used for those who startle easily and feel fearful.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Liu Wei Di Huang Wan
items.description: The foundational TCM Kidney Yin tonic formula. Used for night sweats, hot flashes, insomnia, and tinnitus.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Ba Zhen Wan (Eight Treasure Pill)
items.description: A TCM formula that tonifies both Qi and Blood. Used for fatigue, pallor, and emotional depletion.
items.cautions: Consult a TCM practitioner

items.name: Triphala
items.description: The foundational Ayurvedic formula of three fruits. Supports digestion, detoxification, and overall health.
items.cautions: May cause loose stools initially; reduce dose if so

items.name: Guduchi (Tinospora Cordifolia)
items.description: Known as Amrita (divine nectar) in Ayurveda, guduchi is a powerful immunomodulator and adaptogen.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may lower blood sugar

items.name: Amalaki (Amla)
items.description: One of the richest natural sources of Vitamin C. A Rasayana herb that rejuvenates all tissues.
items.cautions: Generally very safe

items.name: Kapikacchu (Mucuna Pruriens)
items.description: A natural source of L-DOPA, the precursor to dopamine. Used in Ayurveda for nervous system support and mood.
items.cautions: Do not combine with MAOIs or Parkinson medications; consult a doctor

items.name: Haritaki
items.description: One of the three fruits of Triphala. Called the King of Herbs in Ayurveda. Supports digestion, detoxification, and cognitive function.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy; may cause loose stools

items.name: Gokshura (Tribulus)
items.description: An Ayurvedic herb for Kidney and reproductive health. Supports hormonal balance, libido, and vitality.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy

items.name: St. John's Wort
items.description: One of the most studied herbs for mild to moderate depression. Has evidence comparable to SSRIs for mild depression with fewer side effects.
items.cautions: SIGNIFICANT drug interactions — interacts with SSRIs, birth control, blood thinners. Consult your doctor.

items.name: Lavender Oral (Silexan)
items.description: Oral lavender oil (Silexan) has strong clinical evidence for generalized anxiety disorder, comparable to lorazepam.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may cause mild digestive upset

items.name: Hops (Humulus Lupulus)
items.description: A classic nervine herb for anxiety and insomnia. Works well in combination with valerian and passionflower.
items.cautions: May cause drowsiness; avoid with alcohol and sedatives

items.name: Chamomile
items.description: A gentle nervine with evidence for generalized anxiety disorder. Safe for long-term use.
items.dosage: 220-1100mg extract daily, or 2-3 cups of tea
items.cautions: Avoid if allergic to ragweed family plants

items.name: Oat Straw (Avena Sativa)
items.description: A nutritive nervine that nourishes and restores the nervous system over time. Particularly effective for nervous exhaustion and burnout.
items.dosage: 1-3ml tincture daily, or 2-3 cups of infusion
items.cautions: Generally very safe; avoid if gluten-sensitive

items.name: Blue Vervain
items.description: A nervine herb for those who are wound tight — perfectionists and overachievers. Releases physical and mental rigidity.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid during pregnancy

items.name: Lavender Essential Oil
items.description: Aromatherapy with lavender has evidence for reducing anxiety and improving sleep.
items.dosage: Diffuse 3-5 drops; apply diluted to temples or wrists
items.cautions: Dilute before skin application; keep away from eyes

items.name: Inositol (Myo-Inositol)
items.description: A B-vitamin-like compound with strong evidence for OCD, panic disorder, and PCOS.
items.dosage: 12-18g daily for OCD/panic; 2-4g for general support
items.cautions: Generally safe; may cause digestive upset at high doses

items.name: Melatonin (Low Dose)
items.description: The sleep hormone. Low doses (0.5-1mg) are more effective than high doses for most people.
items.dosage: 0.5-1mg 30-60 minutes before bed; less is more
items.cautions: Use lowest effective dose; higher doses can cause grogginess

items.name: Glycine
items.description: An inhibitory amino acid that improves sleep quality and has calming effects on the nervous system.
items.cautions: Very safe; pleasant sweet taste

items.name: Taurine
items.description: An amino acid that acts as a GABA agonist and has calming, neuroprotective effects.
items.cautions: Generally very safe

items.name: Curcumin (BCM-95)
items.description: Highly bioavailable curcumin has evidence for depression, neuroinflammation, and BDNF support.
items.cautions: May interact with blood thinners; use bioavailable forms only

items.name: Psychobiotic Probiotics
items.description: Specific probiotic strains with evidence for mental health. The gut-brain axis is a key pathway in trauma recovery.
items.dosage: Multi-strain probiotic with 10-50 billion CFU daily
items.cautions: Start low to avoid digestive upset; refrigerate

items.name: SAM-e
items.description: A naturally occurring compound with evidence for depression comparable to antidepressants.
items.dosage: 400-1600mg daily on empty stomach; start low
items.cautions: Can cause anxiety or mania in bipolar; do not combine with SSRIs or MAOIs

items.name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR)
items.description: A form of L-carnitine that crosses the blood-brain barrier. Supports mitochondrial function and has evidence for depression.
items.cautions: Generally safe

items.name: Lithium Orotate (Low Dose)
items.description: Low-dose lithium orotate has emerging evidence for neuroprotection and mood stabilization.
items.cautions: Do not confuse with prescription lithium carbonate; consult a doctor

items.name: Iron Bisglycinate
items.description: Iron deficiency is a common and overlooked cause of fatigue, anxiety, and cognitive impairment.
items.dosage: 25-50mg elemental iron daily with Vitamin C; test levels first
items.cautions: Test iron levels before supplementing; excess iron is harmful

items.name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
items.description: Essential for mitochondrial energy production. Depleted by statins and aging.
items.cautions: Generally safe; use ubiquinol form for better absorption

items.name: Mindsight
items.description: Daniel Siegel's accessible introduction to interpersonal neurobiology.
items.author: Daniel J. Siegel, MD

items.name: Running on Empty
items.description: Jonice Webb's groundbreaking work on childhood emotional neglect.
items.author: Jonice Webb, PhD

items.name: The Tapping Solution
items.description: Nick Ortner's accessible guide to EFT tapping.
items.author: Nick Ortner

items.name: The Myth of Normal
items.description: Gabor Mate's comprehensive exploration of how trauma underlies most chronic illness.
items.author: Gabor Maté, MD

items.name: Trauma-Sensitive Mindfulness
items.description: David Treleaven's essential guide to adapting mindfulness for trauma survivors.
items.author: David A. Treleaven

items.name: Overcoming Trauma through Yoga
items.description: David Emerson's guide to trauma-sensitive yoga.
items.author: David Emerson

items.name: Breath: The New Science of a Lost Art
items.description: James Nestor's exploration of the science of breathing.
items.author: James Nestor

items.name: Attached
items.description: Amir Levine's accessible guide to adult attachment theory.
items.author: Amir Levine and Rachel Heller

items.name: It Didn't Start with You
items.description: Mark Wolynn's exploration of inherited family trauma.
items.author: Mark Wolynn

items.name: In the Realm of Hungry Ghosts
items.description: Gabor Mate's compassionate exploration of addiction as a trauma response.
items.author: Gabor Maté, MD

items.name: The Polyvagal Theory in Therapy
items.description: Deb Dana's clinical guide to applying polyvagal theory in therapy.
items.author: Deb Dana

items.name: The Wim Hof Method
items.description: Wim Hof's guide to cold exposure and breathwork for nervous system resilience.
items.author: Wim Hof

items.name: HeartMath Inner Balance Sensor
items.description: A biofeedback device that measures HRV and guides you to a coherent state.
items.usage: Use for 5-20 minutes daily; follow the app guidance
items.cautions: Very safe; requires smartphone

items.name: EMDR Bilateral Tapper
items.description: A handheld bilateral stimulation device used in EMDR therapy.
items.usage: Use with EMDR protocols; ideally with a trained EMDR therapist
items.cautions: Best used with professional guidance

items.name: Grounding / Earthing Mat
items.description: Earthing mats connect you to the earth's electrical field while indoors.
items.usage: Use while sleeping or sitting; follow manufacturer instructions
items.cautions: Ensure proper grounding

items.name: Percussion Massage Gun
items.description: Percussion massage releases myofascial tension and helps discharge stored tension from the body.
items.usage: Use on tense muscles for 1-2 minutes; avoid joints and spine
items.cautions: Avoid on acute injuries or directly over the spine

items.name: Infrared Sauna Blanket
items.description: Infrared heat therapy supports detoxification, muscle relaxation, and mood support.
items.usage: Use for 30-45 minutes; stay hydrated; start at lower temperatures
items.cautions: Stay hydrated; avoid if pregnant or with certain heart conditions

items.name: Yoga Bolster
items.description: A yoga bolster supports restorative yoga poses that activate the parasympathetic nervous system.
items.usage: Use in supported restorative poses; hold for 5-20 minutes
items.cautions: Very safe; ensure comfortable support

items.name: Zafu Meditation Cushion
items.description: A proper meditation cushion supports comfortable seated meditation with proper spinal alignment.
items.usage: Sit on the front third of the cushion; allow hips to be higher than knees
items.cautions: Very safe; adjust height as needed

items.name: Weighted Eye Mask
items.description: A weighted eye mask provides gentle pressure activating the oculocardiac reflex for parasympathetic activation.
items.usage: Use during sleep or rest
items.cautions: Very safe; ensure it is not too heavy

items.name: Neti Pot (Nasal Irrigation)
items.description: Nasal irrigation activates the vagus nerve through the nasal passages and supports respiratory health.
items.usage: Use with sterile saline solution; follow manufacturer instructions carefully
items.cautions: Use ONLY sterile or distilled water; never tap water

items.name: Cold Plunge Tub
items.description: Cold water immersion activates the vagus nerve, releases norepinephrine, and can rapidly shift nervous system state.
items.usage: Start with cold showers; work up to 2-5 minute cold immersion
items.cautions: Not for cardiovascular conditions; never alone

items.name: Selenium
items.description: An essential trace mineral for thyroid function, antioxidant defense, and mood. Deficiency is linked to depression and anxiety.
items.cautions: Do not exceed 400mcg daily; toxicity is possible

items.name: Iodine (Lugols or Potassium Iodide)
items.description: Essential for thyroid hormone production. Deficiency causes fatigue, depression, and cognitive impairment. Test thyroid function before supplementing.
items.cautions: Test thyroid function before supplementing; can worsen autoimmune thyroid conditions

items.name: Potassium (Citrate)
items.description: Essential electrolyte for nerve and muscle function. Deficiency causes fatigue, muscle cramps, and anxiety.
items.cautions: Do not exceed 99mg in supplements without medical supervision; kidney conditions require caution

items.name: Calcium + Magnesium Complex
items.description: The classic calcium-magnesium combination for bone health, muscle relaxation, and sleep. Magnesium is the key active ingredient for nervous system support.
items.cautions: Calcium can interfere with iron absorption; take separately from iron

items.name: Boron
items.description: A trace mineral that supports testosterone, estrogen, and brain function. Deficiency is associated with cognitive decline and hormonal imbalance.
items.cautions: Generally safe at recommended doses

items.name: L-Tyrosine
items.description: A precursor to dopamine, norepinephrine, and thyroid hormones. Particularly helpful for those with low motivation, cognitive fatigue, and stress-induced depletion.
items.cautions: Avoid with MAOIs; can be overstimulating; cycle 5 days on, 2 days off

items.name: L-Tryptophan
items.description: An essential amino acid that converts to serotonin and melatonin. Gentler than 5-HTP. Supports mood, sleep, and anxiety.
items.cautions: Do NOT combine with SSRIs or MAOIs; take away from protein meals

items.name: DL-Phenylalanine (DLPA)
items.description: An amino acid that supports dopamine and endorphin production. Has evidence for depression and chronic pain.
items.cautions: Avoid with PKU; avoid with MAOIs; can be stimulating

items.name: L-Glutamine
items.description: The most abundant amino acid in the body. Supports gut lining integrity, immune function, and is a precursor to GABA.
items.dosage: 5-15g daily; can be taken with food
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid in liver disease or seizure disorders

items.name: L-Arginine
items.description: A precursor to nitric oxide, which supports cardiovascular health and blood flow. Also supports growth hormone release.
items.cautions: Avoid in herpes outbreaks; may interact with blood pressure medications

items.name: Vitamin C (Liposomal)
items.description: Essential for adrenal function, collagen synthesis, and antioxidant defense. The adrenal glands have the highest concentration of Vitamin C in the body.
items.dosage: 500-2000mg daily; liposomal form for best absorption
items.cautions: High doses may cause digestive upset; reduce if so

items.name: Vitamin B Complex
items.description: B vitamins are essential for energy production, neurotransmitter synthesis, and nervous system function. Deficiency is common in those under chronic stress.
items.dosage: As directed on label; take with food
items.cautions: B6 toxicity possible at very high doses; use balanced complex

items.name: Vitamin B12 (Methylcobalamin)
items.description: Essential for nerve function, DNA synthesis, and red blood cell production. Deficiency causes fatigue, depression, and neurological symptoms. Use methylcobalamin form.
items.cautions: Very safe; use methylcobalamin not cyanocobalamin; vegans need to supplement

items.name: Folate (Methylfolate)
items.description: Active folate (5-MTHF) is essential for methylation, neurotransmitter synthesis, and mood. Those with MTHFR gene variants need methylfolate, not folic acid.
items.cautions: Use methylfolate (5-MTHF) not folic acid; start low if MTHFR variant

items.name: Vitamin B6 (P5P Form)
items.description: The active form of B6 (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) is essential for GABA, serotonin, and dopamine synthesis. Important for anxiety and mood.
items.cautions: Do not exceed 100mg daily; high doses of regular B6 can cause neuropathy

items.name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
items.description: Essential partner to Vitamin D3. Directs calcium to bones and away from arteries. Take with D3 for optimal effect.
items.cautions: May interact with blood thinners; take with D3

items.name: Krill Oil
items.description: Omega-3s from krill are bound to phospholipids, making them more bioavailable than fish oil. Also contains astaxanthin.
items.cautions: Avoid if shellfish allergy; may thin blood

items.name: Algae Omega-3 (Vegan)
items.description: The original source of omega-3s (fish get theirs from algae). The vegan alternative to fish oil with equivalent DHA content.
items.cautions: Generally very safe

items.name: Wild Lettuce (Lactuca Virosa)
items.description: A nervine and mild analgesic herb. Used for anxiety, insomnia, and pain. Sometimes called opium lettuce, though it contains no opiates.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid during pregnancy; may cause drowsiness

items.name: Bacopa Monnieri
items.description: An Ayurvedic herb with strong evidence for cognitive function, memory, and reducing anxiety. Works best with consistent use over 8-12 weeks.
items.cautions: May cause digestive upset; take with food; effects build over 8-12 weeks

items.name: Gotu Kola
items.description: An Ayurvedic and TCM herb that supports cognitive function, wound healing, and reduces anxiety. Particularly effective for those with anxiety and cognitive fog.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid during pregnancy; may cause photosensitivity

items.name: Lion's Mane Mushroom
items.description: A medicinal mushroom with strong evidence for nerve growth factor (NGF) stimulation, cognitive function, and mild antidepressant effects.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may cause digestive upset; avoid if mushroom allergy

items.name: Acupressure Mat (Shakti Mat)
items.description: An acupressure mat stimulates thousands of acupressure points, releasing endorphins and promoting deep relaxation. Used for stress, back pain, and sleep.
items.usage: Lie on mat for 10-30 minutes; start with a thin layer of clothing
items.cautions: Avoid on broken skin; start gradually; may be intense initially

items.name: TENS Unit (Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation)
items.description: A TENS unit delivers mild electrical impulses that can reduce pain, release endorphins, and support nervous system regulation.
items.usage: Follow manufacturer instructions; place electrodes on tense or painful areas
items.cautions: Do not use on the head, neck, or over the heart; avoid if pacemaker

items.name: Light Therapy Lamp (10,000 Lux)
items.description: Light therapy has strong evidence for seasonal affective disorder (SAD) and circadian rhythm regulation. Supports cortisol awakening response and serotonin production.
items.usage: Use for 20-30 minutes in the morning within 1 hour of waking
items.cautions: Avoid in bipolar disorder without medical supervision; do not look directly at the light

items.name: Blue Light Blocking Glasses
items.description: Blue light blocking glasses protect melatonin production in the evening. Essential for those with sleep disruption from screen use.
items.usage: Wear 2-3 hours before bed when using screens
items.cautions: Very safe; quality varies between brands

items.name: White Noise Machine
items.description: White noise masks disruptive sounds and creates a consistent auditory environment for sleep. Particularly helpful for hypervigilant trauma survivors who are easily startled.
items.usage: Use at a moderate volume; place away from the bed
items.cautions: Very safe; avoid very high volumes

items.name: Oura Ring Sleep Tracker
items.description: The Oura Ring tracks HRV, sleep stages, body temperature, and recovery. Provides data for understanding how stress and lifestyle affect nervous system recovery.
items.usage: Wear continuously; review morning readiness score and sleep data
items.cautions: Very safe; data should inform not obsess

items.name: Trauma-Informed Journal
items.description: Expressive writing has strong evidence for processing trauma, reducing PTSD symptoms, and improving immune function. A quality journal supports consistent practice.
items.usage: Write for 15-20 minutes; focus on feelings and thoughts; do not edit
items.cautions: Very safe; go slowly with intense trauma; have support available

items.name: Essential Oil Diffuser
items.description: An ultrasonic diffuser disperses essential oils into the air for aromatherapy. The olfactory system has a direct pathway to the limbic system, making aromatherapy a powerful nervous system tool.
items.usage: Add 5-10 drops of essential oil to water; run for 30-60 minutes
items.cautions: Avoid around pets (especially cats); use therapeutic grade oils

items.name: Healing Developmental Trauma
items.description: Laurence Heller's NARM (NeuroAffective Relational Model) approach to healing developmental trauma.
items.author: Laurence Heller and Aline LaPierre

items.name: Somatic Experiencing: Healing Trauma
items.description: Peter Levine's foundational text on Somatic Experiencing therapy.
items.author: Peter A. Levine

items.name: Trauma and Memory
items.description: Peter Levine's exploration of how trauma is stored in the body as implicit memory.
items.author: Peter A. Levine

items.name: Touching Enlightenment
items.description: Reginald Ray's guide to body-based meditation for awakening.
items.author: Reginald A. Ray

items.name: Polyvagal Safety
items.description: Stephen Porges' collection of essays on polyvagal theory and its applications.
items.author: Stephen W. Porges

items.name: The Body Keeps the Score Workbook
items.description: A companion workbook to Bessel van der Kolk's landmark book, with exercises for trauma recovery.
items.author: Bessel van der Kolk, MD

items.name: The Complex PTSD Workbook
items.description: Arielle Schwartz's practical workbook for complex PTSD recovery.
items.author: Arielle Schwartz, PhD

items.name: Healing Your Attachment Wounds
items.description: Diane Poole Heller's guide to healing attachment trauma and building secure relationships.
items.author: Diane Poole Heller

items.name: Will I Ever Be Good Enough?
items.description: Karyl McBride's guide for daughters of narcissistic mothers.
items.author: Karyl McBride, PhD

items.name: Toxic Parents
items.description: Susan Forward's guide to overcoming the legacy of toxic parenting.
items.author: Susan Forward, PhD

items.name: Set Boundaries, Find Peace
items.description: Nedra Tawwab's practical guide to setting healthy boundaries.
items.author: Nedra Glover Tawwab

items.name: Codependent No More
items.description: Melody Beattie's classic guide to recovering from codependency.
items.author: Melody Beattie

items.name: Disarming the Narcissist
items.description: Wendy Behary's guide to navigating relationships with narcissistic people.
items.author: Wendy T. Behary

items.name: Self-Compassion: The Proven Power
items.description: Kristin Neff's foundational work on self-compassion as a healing practice.
items.author: Kristin Neff, PhD

items.name: Fierce Self-Compassion
items.description: Kristin Neff's exploration of the fierce, protective side of self-compassion.
items.author: Kristin Neff, PhD

items.name: The Mindful Self-Compassion Workbook
items.description: Kristin Neff and Chris Germer's practical workbook for developing self-compassion.
items.author: Kristin Neff and Christopher Germer

items.name: Radical Acceptance
items.description: Tara Brach's guide to embracing your life with the heart of a Buddha.
items.author: Tara Brach, PhD

items.name: True Refuge
items.description: Tara Brach's guide to finding peace and freedom in the midst of difficulty.
items.author: Tara Brach, PhD

items.name: Berberine
items.description: A plant alkaloid with strong evidence for blood sugar regulation, gut health, and metabolic support. Comparable to metformin in some studies.
items.cautions: May lower blood sugar; avoid with diabetes medications without monitoring; avoid during pregnancy

items.name: Black Seed Oil (Nigella Sativa)
items.description: A traditional Islamic medicine herb with evidence for immune support, anti-inflammatory effects, and mood improvement.
items.cautions: Generally safe; may interact with blood thinners

items.name: Passionflower Tea
items.description: Passionflower tea is a gentle, accessible way to use this nervine herb. Particularly effective for anxiety with racing thoughts before bed.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid during pregnancy; may cause drowsiness

items.name: Valerian + Hops + Passionflower
items.description: The classic nervine triple combination for anxiety and insomnia. Each herb works through different mechanisms for synergistic effect.
items.dosage: As directed on combination product label; take 30-60 minutes before bed
items.cautions: May cause drowsiness; avoid with alcohol and sedatives

items.name: Lemon Balm Tea
items.description: Lemon balm tea is a gentle, accessible nervine for anxiety and sleep. Particularly effective for anxiety with digestive symptoms.
items.cautions: Generally very safe; avoid with thyroid conditions

items.name: Magnesium Threonate (Magtein)
items.description: The only form of magnesium shown to cross the blood-brain barrier. Specifically supports cognitive function and memory.
items.cautions: Generally safe; more expensive than other forms

items.name: Lion's Mane Coffee Blend
items.description: A blend of coffee with Lion's Mane mushroom extract. Provides cognitive benefits of both caffeine and NGF stimulation.
items.cautions: Contains caffeine; avoid after 2pm

items.name: Mushroom Complex (7 Species)
items.description: A comprehensive blend of medicinal mushrooms including Reishi, Lion Mane, Chaga, Turkey Tail, Cordyceps, Shiitake, and Maitake.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid if mushroom allergy

items.name: Neem
items.description: An Ayurvedic herb with powerful antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and blood-purifying properties.
items.cautions: Avoid during pregnancy; may lower blood sugar

items.name: Moringa Leaf
items.description: A nutrient-dense superfood with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and adaptogenic properties.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid during pregnancy at high doses

items.name: Spirulina
items.description: A blue-green algae superfood rich in protein, B vitamins, iron, and antioxidants.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid if autoimmune condition; check for heavy metals in source

items.name: Chlorella
items.description: A green algae superfood with detoxification, immune support, and heavy metal chelation properties.
items.cautions: Start low to avoid detox reactions; check source for quality

items.name: Digestive Enzymes
items.description: Digestive enzyme supplements support food breakdown and nutrient absorption. Helpful for those with chronic stress, which suppresses digestive function.
items.cautions: Generally safe; avoid with active ulcers

items.name: Betaine HCl
items.description: Supports stomach acid production, which is often low in those with chronic stress. Low stomach acid impairs protein digestion and nutrient absorption.
items.dosage: 650mg with protein-containing meals; start with one and increase
items.cautions: Do NOT use with active ulcers or NSAID use; test with one capsule first

items.name: Slippery Elm
items.description: A demulcent herb that soothes and coats the digestive tract. Used for IBS, GERD, and the gut-brain connection in trauma survivors.
items.dosage: 400-500mg capsules 3x daily, or 1 tablespoon powder in water
items.cautions: Generally very safe; take away from medications as it may slow absorption

items.name: Licorice Root (DGL)
items.description: DGL (deglycyrrhizinated licorice) supports adrenal function and soothes the digestive tract.
items.cautions: Use DGL form only; whole licorice can raise blood pressure

items.name: Collagen Peptides
items.description: Hydrolyzed collagen supports gut lining integrity, joint health, skin, and provides glycine for sleep and nervous system support.
items.cautions: Generally very safe; choose grass-fed sources

items.name: Psyllium Husk
items.description: A soluble fiber that supports gut microbiome health, blood sugar regulation, and cholesterol.
items.cautions: Take with plenty of water; start low to avoid gas; take away from medications
